referenced_section_hint: Securitisation — operational requirements for regulatory capital relief
description: APS 220 excludes from its scope exposures securitised or transferred into securitisation vehicles that meet APRA's operational requirements for regulatory capital relief under APS 120.
Terms defined in APS 001 appear in bold on first use in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Risk Management — overall risk appetite statement and risk management strategy
description: APS 220 credit risk appetite statement and credit risk management strategy need not be stand-alone documents where they form part of the overall risk appetite statement or risk management strategy required under CPS 220.
ADIs that outsource aspects of credit risk management must have regard to CPS 231 for outsourced functions.
For large exposures, ADIs must also have regard to APS 221 Large Exposures.
For associations with related entities, ADIs must also have regard to APS 222 Associations with Related Entities.

referenced_section_hint: Governance — senior officer requirements for foreign ADIs
description: Board requirements for foreign ADIs must be read subject to CPS 510, which requires a foreign ADI to nominate a senior officer outside Australia with delegated authority.

referenced_section_hint: Risk Management — internal risk appetite limits
description: In addition to requirements in CPS 220, APS 221 and APS 222, ADIs must develop policies to ensure adequate portfolio diversification.

referenced_section_hint: Large Exposures — internal risk appetite limits
description: In addition to requirements in CPS 220, APS 221 and APS 222, ADIs must develop policies to ensure adequate portfolio diversification.

referenced_section_hint: Associations with Related Entities — internal risk appetite limits
description: In addition to requirements in CPS 220, APS 221 and APS 222, ADIs must develop policies to ensure adequate portfolio diversification.
Paragraph 46 refers to Attachment A for detailed requirements on collateral valuation policies and processes.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Counterparty Credit Risk — standardised approach and adjusted current exposure method
description: For derivative transaction exposures, ADIs must calculate exposures for non-performance measurement under the standardised approach or adjusted current exposure method prescribed in APS 180.
Paragraph 108 refers to Attachment B for the prescribed provisioning approach that APRA may permit or require ADIs to implement.

referenced_section_hint: eligible lenders mortgage insurer definition and eligible collateral provisions
description: Attachment B references APS 112 for the definition of eligible lenders mortgage insurer and for the eligible collateral provisions relevant to cash on deposit deductions in prescribed provisioning.
